title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
summary: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 with Claude, suggesting an alternative approach. The video promotes his coding education community and hiring information while referencing tools like AutoGit and Glaido.
writeup: A hands-on workflow guide for getting the most out of Claude 4.5 in the first 14 hours after its release, focused on where to run the model, how to avoid unnecessary safety-filter trips, and the stark economics of subscription versus API pricing. The creator argues that the Claude app and raw API are both worse defaults than Cursor's agent view or Claude Code, and that Anthropic's June 23 decision to pull Claude 4.5 from subscription plans will sharply widen the gap between high-spend practitioners and everyone else. The through-line is that capability gains from frontier models only compound for users who also master the operational layer around them.
key_points: Cursor's agent view and Claude Code are the recommended interfaces for Claude 4.5: they handle safety-filter trips gracefully by falling back to Opus 4.8, whereas raw API calls simply throw errors with no retry, and the Claude app carries heavier guardrails tuned for a mass consumer audience.

Claude 4.5 trips its safety filters on surprisingly mundane prompts, including asking it to explain itself comprehensively or auditing an app for security vulnerabilities; the workaround is to run the sensitive analysis through a different model first, then pass the output to Claude 4.5 in XML tags in a fresh conversation window.

Inside Claude Code, the $100 or $200 per month Max subscription delivers roughly 8 to 10 times the effective token volume compared to equivalent API spend on OpenRouter, because Anthropic subsidizes the subscription tier; the creator's own OpenRouter bill ran $6K in a single month before Claude 4.5 launched.

Anthropic announced removal of Claude 4.5 from subscription plans on June 23, meaning subscribers will have to pay full API-rate pricing afterward; the creator frames this as the beginning of a meaningful capability gap between practitioners spending thousands per month and those who cannot.

Claude 4.5 at extra-high effort substantially outperforms earlier models on complex refactoring tasks: it one-shots canvas bugs that GPT and prior Claude versions failed to resolve across multiple attempts, and it proactively runs browser renders and multi-angle tests before declaring a change done.
